back_translation: Solomon the royal son of David established his kingdom firmly, and the LORD his God was with him and exalted him exceedingly high.
Solomon spoke to all Israel — to the commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds, to the judges, and to every leader in Israel, the heads of the families.

Then Solomon, together with the whole assembly, went to the high place at Gibeon, because that was the location of the Tent of Meeting of God, which Moses the servant of the LORD had built in the wilderness.
As for the ark of God, David had brought it up from Kiriath-jearim to the place that he had prepared for it, for he had pitched a tent for that ark in Jerusalem.
But the bronze altar that Bezalel son of Uri, son of Hur, had made stood at Gibeon before the tabernacle of the LORD; so Solomon and the assembly went to seek Him there.
Solomon went up to the bronze altar that was at the Tent of Meeting, before the presence of the LORD, and offered a thousand burnt offerings on that altar.
That night God appeared to Solomon and said, “Ask! What do you want Me to give you?”
Solomon said to God, “You have shown great steadfast love to David my royal father, and have made me king in his place.
Now, O LORD God, let the promise that You gave to David my royal father be fulfilled, for You have made me king over a people as numerous as the dust of the earth.
Now grant me wisdom and knowledge, that I may lead this people out and in; for who can govern this great people of Yours?”

Then God said to Solomon, “Because this was in your heart, and you did not ask for riches, wealth, or honor, nor did you ask for the life of those who hate you, and you did not ask for long life, but you asked for wisdom and knowledge for yourself, to govern My people over whom I have made you king —
therefore I have given you wisdom and knowledge; and I will also give you riches, wealth, and honor, such as no king before you has received, and no king after you will receive the like.”
Then Solomon went from the high place at Gibeon, from before the Tent of Meeting, back to Jerusalem, and he reigned over Israel.
Solomon gathered chariots and horsemen; he had one thousand four hundred chariots and twelve thousand horses, which he stationed in the chariot cities and with the king at Jerusalem.
The king made silver and gold in Jerusalem as plentiful as stones, and made cedar wood as plentiful as the sycamore trees in the foothills.
Solomon's horses were imported from Egypt and from Kue; the royal merchants bought the horses from Kue at a price.

A single chariot imported from Egypt cost six hundred shekels of silver, and a horse one hundred fifty shekels. Likewise, they exported these horses and chariots to all the kings of the Hittites and the kings of Aram through the hand of those merchants.
Solomon resolved to build a house for the name of the LORD, and to build a royal palace for his kingdom.
